title: Pouteria aylmeri
intro: Pouteria aylmeri, also known as the Babing, is a tropical tree native to Australia and New Guinea, belonging to the family Sapotaceae. The bark and root of Pouteria aylmeri have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Pouteria aylmeri include flavonoids, saponins, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Pouteria aylmeri is commonly used in the form of a tincture, infusion, or decoction, often combined with other plants to enhance its therapeutic effects.
taxonomy: Pouteria aylmeri is a species of tree in the family Sapotaceae. It is native to Madagascar and belongs to the genus Pouteria, which consists of tropical trees and shrubs. The family Sapotaceae is a group of flowering plants that are commonly known as sapotes. This family comprises over 900 species, including the sapote, mamey, and chocolate tree. The classification of Pouteria aylmeri reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history as a member of this family.
